message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hmm? that sounds strange. @PLAYER can you take point and start sizing this up?
sure thing, I'll take command of the incident
:star: Take command
Thanks Andy - Let me know if you need me
@bob can you please give us a precise list of the customers and what they are each reporting?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Checking reports...
let's get a few people in here to help out
@hamed , @daniel can you join us?
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
Hi Andy, i am current away from desk, can you call tanya
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
i should be back soon
sure Hamed, @tanya, can you join us please?
Hi Andy
:star: Escalating when required
what's the issue?
@hamed can you give an ETA on your availability?
:star: Manage actions and timings
aroudn 5
5 minutes
I dont see any issue in platform - what do we have
@daniel, some customer reports of an issue
Sure, i ll check - But i can place orders in website
thanks, @bob have you been able to reproduce yourself?
:star: Reproduces the incident
Gathering more information....
@daniel what were your reproduction steps?
The customers are complaining that the image is not matching up with the product name
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
I can see there is an image of sunglasses against a tank top in the site
Hi @Andy - is something wrong ? i saw few posts on social media complaints
that looks wrong
hi Bez, it looks like we have an issue with the site - I will declare an incident and we'll get on it
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
sure
looks like  we have enough to declare this an incident
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
@PLAYER Could you please discuss this with Bez?
*Incident Number INC100096:* P2 Customers potentially ordering incorrect products
:star: Incident record created
I'm receiving more cancellation/refund requests than new orders. @PLAYER What shall we do now?
How big is this problem?
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
unclear - it appears that customers might be ordering the wrong products
@Andy, do we know what products are customer buying? one on image or label ?
:star: Qualify the problem
I'm concerned we might be doing more harm than good by letting the new orders continue...
@bob can you put up a status page explaining that we're having some issues with orders
Orders are still coming in high volume, and we now have 130+ customers asking to cancel their orders
What's the impact?
@Daniel ? can you do that ? if we can styop orders till then
yeah, we can bring a maintenance page
But that might stop all orders?
is that okay ?
that's fair, we are receiving a lot of refund requests - we've put up a status page for customers to clarify for now
please prepare that @daniel but don't put it up just yet
Sure
Lets stop all orders untill w clear
@daniel can you look into what changes have been shipped recently
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
Thanks @shay - @daniel could you review the changes and see if any explain the issue we're seeing
:star: Reviewed changes
Sure me and @Shay can take a look now.
@bob can you confirm the statement that the orders are correct and we're sending the right item? is it just the confirmation that's wrong?
CHG89415: Checked the mobile Add to Cart button is working perfectly for me
For CHG89418 the CartService 3.9.3 deployed successfully, no functional issues detected.
And lastly CHG89412: product names in Redis match UAT DB
it's possible that it's just the pictures that are wrong, we have a report from shipping that the orders are being placed correctly and the right item being shipped
:star: Facts over opinion
@bob
Yeah - but do we know if cuastomer is placing orders based on label or image
@bob can you confirm whether any customers have received the wrong item having ordered it
We are not sure what orders are being taken - not sure if its image or label -
Thats still unsure
if that hypothesis is true, I propose we put a notice up saying that pictures are incorrect - that would allow customers to continue to use the site
We are taking more and more orders - atleast 50 to 60 orders per minute
@bez would you be happy with that, or would you rather we took the site down still?
Look slike there will be lot of returns and courier fright we will have to absorb
@bob can you place an order and verify whether it's the image or the text that is being ordered?
Again, your call @Andy
i suppose it a good deal to stop orders now
Considering what Bob just told me
I did check - sometimes, its image
sometime its label
CHG89412 sounds suspicious
okay, thanks Bez - customer impact and potential knock on effects sound significant
@daniel go ahead and put the site in maintenance mode
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
ah thanks for the clarification
yes, pause new orders I'll escalate to P1
Let me check if there are any errors
I can take a look into our services now
parallely working on maintenance page
*Incident Number INC10000:* P1 Orders paused while investigating catalog issues
We don't have any alerts in the system
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
Even i don't see any issue
@Andy, the maintenance page is up
Must be network or platform issue.
@shay what are you looking at for issues?
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
I looked all apps health - they are healthy
@PLAYER How does it look now?
@Tanya
Checking now...
@daniel could it be the case that the UAT populated the redis cache incorrectly in production?
@PLAYER Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
no memory or cpu spikes all looks good from Infra nothing to highlight
Unlikely to be - i will check if UAT is working fine
Although I don't have any data, I strongly believe this issue originates from the application rather than the platform.
No spikes or response time deviations, app owners must check configuration.
This is not an infra problem. If users are seeing mismatched products, someone could be messing with our system otherwise its an application/service-level bug
I will post an update here in 10 minutes when we have investigated further
Thanks
we have some hypotheses
:star: Track actions and time box them
But it's not a platform issue.
Well, even we dont see any errors @Tanya, its not likely to be App issue
can you give me a detailed report of what change CHG89412 introducesd?
Hi @Andy, i am here
what are we upto
yes, 1 minute
Ran a UAT DB update doing data refresh

@bob can you update our service page and tell customers that if they ordered anything recently there might be an issue, we are temporarily not taking further orders and we're working on a resolution - update in 10 minutes
Noted thanks
@daniel could that possibly impact production?
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
i am sure i ran it on UAT- but checking that
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
@PLAYER if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
do we have a backup of the database?
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @PLAYER
We have it on Canonical DB @Andy
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
what do you want to know, @bez?
Ok here is what i found
Ignore that please
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
when did we first receive customer reports of an issue @bob
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Go ahead with investigation, i ll check that- There is a lot of pressure from Stakeholders
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
good spot, thanks @daniel
thanks for the info @tanya
what are our options for recovering from this?
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
we have a working theory now that a recent migration was applied in production instead of testing, we are working options
Perfect
:star: Form a working theory
It your call @PLAYER
would clearing the cache and restarting the service have any negative impact?
No, nothing
It shouldn't
@tanya, do you agree?
Restart dont have any impact - but i dont think that will work
okay, but it's safe to try - please give it a go @daniel
Thank you, i will clear cache and bounce the servers
how can we verify if it was successful?
i can check mapping
bu ti am sure, this is wrong Db update- we will have to repopulate the DB
Oh no, the data still appears to be wrong. I believe Tanya might be right
can you confirm that hasn't resolved the issue @tanya ?
Yes, the config still looks th same
can you put together a plan for rehydrating the store, @tanya ?
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
I know every one is busy- Any ETA @Andy
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
@daniel can you confirm that if we take this action the issue won't recur?
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
@PLAYER please verify that all steps have been completed
Are we good to proceed @PLAYER?
we've attempted one quick path to resolution bit that didn't succeed - we are working on restoration steps now
Yes, because we are taking the golden source of truth from canonical DB - we should be good
I will update in 5 minutes
Thanks
does anyone have a reason we shouldn't proceed with these steps?
I have validated, we should be good
@daniel or @shay could one of you prepare to execute step 3
we are good to go
sure
on my tose
okay, @tanya - go ahead with steps 1 and 2
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
Step 1 is now complete
Moving on to step 2
Step 2 complete
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
@daniel can you prepare to verify the fix and prepare to bring the site back online if it works?
Starting step 3.....
Yes
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
@bob can you add an update that we've identified the root case and are working on a resolution, next update in 5 mintues
Okay, noted
@daniel can you verify the fix?
@Tanya, can you check that in DB
i ll check the apps
thanks
@PLAYER Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
is that correct?
Yes, i checked for all products, the mapping looks good
Yes, i have verified
are all our services up and running correctly now?
can we test an order before bringing the site back online?
No, but can you check if the maintenanc epage i sup ?
but i see the data is being fetched correctly
yes the maintenance page is still up
We should be good to remove the banner
evenyone: is there anything else we could do to validate the fix before bringing the site online?
@bez the restore appears to have been successful, we're verifying now
I see the data mapping is alright
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
next step is to bring the site online
if verification is successful
I have validated product by product - mapping is alright
okay, I intend to bring the site back online then
Lets goo
@daniel can you remove the maintenance banner
Maintenance page removal in progress...
let's verify with a real order
:star: Validate the fix
We are LIVE for orders back again!
@bob please keep an eye on customer comms and let us know if any new issues are reported
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
will do
@Andy, can you verify if we are back
@bez we need to recover from some fallout here - would you prefer if we cancelled orders or contacted customers who ordered in that window?
We can refund with an apology note
thanks, that sounds fair
site appears available to me
i can place orders now
ordering a tank top appears to order a tank top
great
@bob Bez confirms refund orders and send apology note
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
@bob
Looks like we are back to business
Perfect thanks
@bob you can take down the status page as well
All done already
@bob
@bob please add a note that anyone who ordered in that period will receive a refund and can contact us for further help
Sure
Great work @Andy
@daniel are you happy that we're back?
Well done everyone
Root cause - UAT data refresh script was accidentally run against prod, overwriting product names in DB and Redis cache with test data. Monitoring missed it as health metrics stayed normal.
This was tricky, much appreciate the help
Thanks @PLAYER and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure
thanks Bez!
